extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello won from 8-rider breakaway on uphill finish after attacking with 1900m to go, exploiting hesitation among chasers.
tactical_patterns: DE PLUS Laurens abandoned after previous day breakaway effort, proving recovery cost from non-productive breaks in Grand Tours.
BILBAO attacked 1900m out when chasers hesitated looking at each other, demonstrating how indecision creates decisive gaps on gradual finales.
PEDRERO Antonio pulled exceptionally hard for ROJAS despite both being in breakaway, revealing Movistar willing to sacrifice one leader for another.
UAE struggled to control after intermediate sprint with only two riders working, forcing BORA to contribute despite no sprint interest.

rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: negative: abandoned day after breakaway work, critical loss for ROGLIČ's mountain support depth
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: timed 1900m attack perfectly when rivals hesitated, showed strong finishing power despite long breakaway
rider_signals.PEDRERO Antonio: positive: pulled aggressively for breakaway companion ROJAS despite own GC ambitions, strong domestique instincts
rider_signals.CONTI Valerio: neutral: pink jersey team required BORA assistance to control, revealing UAE lacks chase depth
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: neutral: third place but unable to follow BILBAO's decisive acceleration with 1900m remaining
